title: composite-loader silently registers nothing in pointerless target dirs (loadContext graceful-degrade) + launch script lacks pointer-presence pre-flight check — operator gets a 'constrained' session with zero composites and no signal
description: Audit (analysis/2026-05-28-launch-script-harness-canonical-audit.md commit 945dc93, gap 9 HIGH + F.4) empirically established: packages/pi-context/src/context.ts loadContext gracefully returns {config: null} when .pi-context.json pointer is absent. packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/composite-loader.ts:82 buildForbiddenUnion(null) returns the framework-only FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS set; composite-loader's iteration of config.tool_operations[] returns []. composite-loader.ts:103 returns {registered: [], skipped: []}. NO ERROR. The launched session sees: the 5 static pi-agent-dispatch tools (post-FGAP-117 closure) + zero composites — operator has no signal that composites are 'missing' vs 'none declared.' Symmetric concern at the launch-script layer: scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh has no pre-flight that checks .pi-context.json existence + asserts config.tool_operations[] population matches operator intent. Operator launches against a fresh project + gets a session with vastly reduced capability + no warning.

evidence.reference: loadContext graceful-degrade path when .pi-context.json absent → returns {config: null}
lines 82-104 — buildForbiddenUnion + iteration silently produce empty registered[] under config:null
no pointer-presence pre-flight; no observability around composite-loader's silent no-op

impact: Operator observability gap. Launching against a pointerless target dir produces a session indistinguishable (from the operator's perspective) from a properly-configured launch with zero declared composites. Silent no-op is anti-canon per DEC-0047 no-deception clause + feedback_no_stderr_diagnostics (silent behavior drops violate canon).
proposed_resolution: Two-layer enforcement per existing canon patterns: (a) composite-loader emits a TraceEntry warning (or returns a 'config_absent' marker in its return shape) when loadContext returns config:null; the extension factory surfaces this via pi.ui.notify (or equivalent observable surface) at startup — matches DEC-0047 no-deception clause + the TraceEntry pattern established for DEC-0002 thinking-seam warning; (b) scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh adds a pre-flight `test -f "$TARGET/.pi-context.json" || warn` step before exec-ing pi — operator gets explicit notification + can decide whether to proceed or run /context init first. Neither layer requires canon evolution; both apply existing patterns (TraceEntry observability + pre-flight ceremony). Lands as part of FEAT-011 implementation cascade. [CLOSED 2026-05-28 via TASK-091 unified (FEAT-006+FEAT-011) merge at task-091-unified-feat-006-feat-011 + VER-057 attestation. See verification body for per-criterion evidence.]
